heading: Conserve Burst Credits
body:
If you regularly exceed your configured IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) threshold, you can avoid
depleting your burst credits by increasing your configured IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) with
any of the following actions:

- [Modify a Cluster](scale-cluster-storage) to increase the
  cluster's IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) threshold.

- [Modify a Cluster](scale-cluster-storage). The
  IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) threshold increases as you increase storage capacity. Clusters
  configured to use at least 1 TB of storage have baseline IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second)
  performance that is equal to or greater than the maximum burst
  performance. These volumes do not deplete burst credit balances.

- [Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance). Larger cluster
  tiers include higher IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) thresholds.

  > **Note:** Cluster tiers `M140` and larger are deployed with at least 1 TB
  > of storage capacity by default. Clusters with 1 TB or more of
  > storage capacity do not deplete burst credit balances.
